extracted_information: Stripe webhooks allow your application to receive real-time event data from your Stripe account, enabling backend systems to react to asynchronous events such as payment confirmations, disputes, or recurring payment success. To implement, you create an HTTPS webhook endpoint to receive POST requests with a JSON Event object payload. It's crucial to quickly return a 2xx status code and defer complex logic. Stripe provides tools for local testing via the CLI and options for registering endpoints via the Dashboard or API. Key best practices include handling duplicate events, listening only to necessary event types, processing events asynchronously, securing endpoints with HTTPS, and periodically rolling signing secrets. For security, signature verification using the `Stripe-Signature` header is essential to confirm events originate from Stripe and to prevent replay attacks by checking the timestamp. Stripe also allows verifying events against a list of known IP addresses. For subscription systems, events like `customer.subscription.created`, `invoice.created`, `invoice.paid`, and `charge.created` are relevant, and the system should not rely on guaranteed event ordering.

specifications.webhook_endpoint_protocol: HTTPS (required for live mode), HTTP (for local development)
specifications.tls_versions_supported: v1.2 or higher (Stripe webhooks support v1.2 and v1.3)

automatic_retry_behavior.live_mode: Up to 3 days with exponential back-off

manual_retry_availability.dashboard: Up to 15 days after event creation
manual_retry_availability.cli: Up to 30 days after event creation

specifications.event_ordering_guarantee: None (Stripe does not guarantee event delivery order)

features.name: Real-time Event Delivery
features.description: Receive events as they occur in your Stripe account.

features.name: Asynchronous Event Handling
features.description: Respond to events like payment confirmations, disputes, and recurring payment successes.

features.name: Local Testing with Stripe CLI
features.description: Test webhook endpoint handlers locally using `stripe listen` to forward events or trigger test events.

features.name: Endpoint Registration
features.description: Register publicly accessible HTTPS URLs for webhook endpoints via Dashboard or API.

features.name: Signature Verification
features.description: Verify that webhook events are sent by Stripe using the `Stripe-Signature` header and endpoint secret (with official libraries or manually).

features.name: Organization Event Handling
features.description: Handle events across multiple accounts within an organization, inspecting the `context` value to determine the originating account.

features.name: Automatic Retries
features.description: Stripe automatically retries event delivery if the endpoint does not return a 2xx status code.

features.name: Manual Retries
features.description: Ability to manually resend events from the Dashboard or Stripe CLI.

features.name: Duplicate Event Handling
features.description: Best practice to guard against processing the same event multiple times by logging event IDs.

features.name: Selective Event Listening
features.description: Configure endpoints to receive only specific event types required by the integration.

features.name: Asynchronous Processing Recommendation
features.description: Advised to process events using an asynchronous queue to prevent scalability issues and timeouts.

features.name: CSRF Protection Exemption
features.description: Guidance on exempting webhook routes from CSRF protection in web frameworks.

features.name: Endpoint Secret Management
features.description: Ability to periodically roll (change) endpoint signing secrets for security.

features.name: IP Address Verification
features.description: Recommendation to trust events only from a set list of known Stripe IP addresses.

features.name: Replay Attack Prevention
features.description: Timestamp in the `Stripe-Signature` header helps mitigate replay attacks by allowing applications to reject old payloads.

temporal_info.retry_durations: Live mode: up to 3 days; Sandbox mode: 3 times over a few hours.
temporal_info.manual_retry_window: Dashboard: up to 15 days; CLI: up to 30 days.
temporal_info.signature_timestamp_tolerance: Default 5 minutes for replay attack prevention.

references.name: Amazon EventBridge as an event destination
